item.title: Boost for Drua Women as Imeri Nai returns from concussion
item.description: There is encouraging news for the McDonald's Fijian Drua Women ahead of the start of their 2026 Super Rugby Women's campaign, with flyhalf Imeri Nai returning to training after recovering from a concussion. Head Coach Mike Legge confirmed to fijivillage News that Nai is back in training following the injury she sustained during the Drua Women's pre-season trial against the NSW Waratahs Women in Su
item.content:encoded:
There is encouraging news for the McDonald's Fijian Drua Women ahead of the start of their 2026 Super Rugby Women's campaign, with flyhalf Imeri Nai returning to training after recovering from a concussion.

Head Coach Mike Legge confirmed to fijivillage News that Nai is back in training following the injury she sustained during the Drua Women's pre-season trial against the NSW Waratahs Women in Suva.

Nai was forced from the field during the match after suffering a concussion and has spent the past few weeks on the sidelines as she progressed through her recovery.

Her return comes as the Drua Women prepare to launch their Super Rugby Women's season this Saturday against the defending champion Waratahs at Leichhardt Oval in Sydney.

Legge has named an exciting squad featuring a blend of experienced internationals and emerging talent.

Several players are set to make their official Super Rugby Women's debuts for the Drua, including Vodafone Fijiana 15s representatives Varanisese Qoro and Ivamere Rokowati, along with Nadroga lock Karavaki Lutumaibau and Nasinu standout Aqela Raitubu.

The starting 15 sees the most capped Drua player of all-time (25 appearances) Karalaini Naisewa (1) at loosehead prop, with Tiana Robanakadavu (3) at tighthead. Captain Bitila Tawake (2) will start at hooker. In the middle row, Vunamoli, Nadi native MereoniNakesa (4) partners the the debuting KaravakiLutumaibau (5). The squad’s youngest member, 18-year-old Carletta Yee (6) lines up at blindside flanker with Nunia Daunimoala (7) at openside. Vice-captain Alfreda Fisher (8) starts the match at number 8. In the backline, the experienced Evivi Senikarivi (9) gets the nod to start at halfback, partnering VaraniseseQoro (10) who is at flyhalf. Flyer Aqela Raitubu (11) starts on the left wing with vice-captain Kolora Lomani (14) on the right wing. Josivini Naihamu (12) starts the match at inside centre with Ivamere Rokowati (13) at outside centre. Litiana Vueti (15) has been handed the fullback duties for the match. For impact this week, Keleni Marawa (16) is backup hooker, Zipporah Sorokacika (17) is loosehead, with Keri Lawavou (18) covering the tighthead prop position. Lanky second rower Vilisi Tivalele (19) will cover for the locks and Salaseini Railumu (20) backs up the loose forward trio. Vatiseva Cavuru (21) will cover the halves with Ruth Raketekete (22) covering the centres. Michella’e Stolz (23) completes matchday squad covering the back three.

item.title: Fiji Men to kick off Bordeaux 7s against Kenya while Fijiana 7s to face Japan first
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 03/06/2026 The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side will open its Bordeaux 7s campaign against Kenya at 12.44am on Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 03/06/2026

The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s side will open its Bordeaux 7s campaign against Kenya at 12.44am on Saturday.

Fiji is currently fourth in the World Rugby Sevens Championship standings with 26 points.

South Africa leads the standings with 38 points, followed by Argentina on 34 points and Australia on 30.

Fiji's second match will be against Great Britain at 6.14am on Saturday, before taking on South Africa at 9.06pm on Saturday in its final pool match.

The men's Cup quarter-finals will begin at 3.28am on Sunday, the semi-finals are scheduled for 11.14pm on Sunday, and the final will kick off at 3.41am on Monday.

Meanwhile, the Fijiana 7s side will begin its campaign in France against Japan at 10.44pm on Friday.

The Fijiana will then face Australia at 3.51am on Saturday before taking on Brazil at 11.32pm on Saturday in its final pool match.

The women's Cup quarter-finals will start at 1.30am on Sunday, the semi-finals are scheduled for 10.30pm on Sunday, and the final will be played at 3.04am on Monday.

item.title: Blues are the arch enemy and will be a threat - Crusaders coach
item.description: Crusaders coach Rob Penney says anyone claiming they will "roll" through the Blues in Saturday's qualifying final is underestimating the Auckland based side. The defending champions have won their last three games (against the Blues, Chiefs and Hurricanes), while the Blues enter the playoffs on the back of three straight defeats.
item.content:encoded:
Crusaders coach Rob Penney says anyone claiming they will "roll" through the Blues in Saturday's qualifying final is underestimating the Auckland based side.

The defending champions have won their last three games (against the Blues, Chiefs and Hurricanes), while the Blues enter the playoffs on the back of three straight defeats.

Despite the odds being against the visitors, Penney insists it will be a tough match.

Penney believes the Blues are still the Crusaders biggest rivals and he is expecting the visitors to be at their best on Saturday.

He says Blues are the arch enemy, to a large degree, and that they will be a threat.

The Crusaders have never lost a playoff game at home, winning 32 in a row, and are favourites to beat the Blues at a sold out Te Kaha Stadium and progress to the semi-finals.

That record is incredibly impressive, but Penney says protecting that unbeaten streak hasn't played much of a role in the Crusaders buildup to Saturday's match.

The Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific Qualifying Final fixtures have been confirmed with the defending champions, the Crusaders, hosting the Blues in Christchurch at 4.35pm Saturday.

The table-topping Hurricanes will kick off the Finals Series when they take on the ACT Brumbies at Hnry Stadium at 7.05pm Friday.

The Chiefs will wrap up the Qualifying Finals with a Saturday night showdown against the Queensland Reds at 7.05pm at FMG Stadium Waikato in Hamilton.

The three winning teams in the Qualifying Finals will progress to the semi-finals along with the highest-ranked losing team (the 'lucky loser').

In a variation from last season, this year the 'lucky loser' will be relegated to the lowest seeding for the semi-finals and, as such, will forego any chance to host the semi-finals or the Grand Final, should they progress.

item.title: Liquidators' report reveals $8.2M hole at Moana Pasifika
item.description: Moana Pasifika is facing more than $8 million in creditor claims, according to the first report from liquidators appointed to the

item.title: All Whites suffer worst loss in nine years in first FIFA World Cup warm-up match
item.description: The All Whites’ worst result in nine years has left them with plenty to digest and plenty to work on less than a fortnight out fro

item.title: New potential saviour emerges for Moana Pasifika
Nabenu set for Rewa, Ali lodges transfer request to Lautoka
item.description: New Zealand-based Extra Bula FC defender Semi Nabenu is set to join Rewa FC after completing the mandatory 30-day stand-down period required for a change from professional to amateur status. Nabenu is expected to strengthen the Delta Tigers’ defensive line as the side prepares for two major competitions, the BiC Fiji FACT and the 2026 OFC Men’s Champions League.
item.content:encoded:
New Zealand-based Extra Bula FC defender Semi Nabenu is set to join Rewa FC after completing the mandatory 30-day stand-down period required for a change from professional to amateur status.

Nabenu is expected to strengthen the Delta Tigers’ defensive line as the side prepares for two major competitions, the BiC Fiji FACT and the 2026 OFC Men’s Champions League.

The side is expected to benefit from Nabenu’s international experience as they aim to build momentum heading into the Fiji FACT.

Rewa is in Group A alongside defending champions Labasa, Suva and Navua in what is expected to be the tournament's most competitive pool.

In a separate development, the Fiji Football Association has confirmed that the first application under the Mid-Season Transfer Window has been received.

Meanwhile, Afraz Ali has expressed an interest in joining Lautoka FC from Nadi FC.

The transfer window runs from 1st June to 15th June , and all applications will be processed in accordance with Fiji FA transfer regulations.

The Fiji FACT will be played from 19th to 21st June at Subrail Park in Labasa.

The semi-finals and final will be held the following week at the same venue.

item.title: Ma'a Nonu honoured with haka following last Toulon appearance
item.description: Former All Black and Hurricanes player Ma'a Nonu may have played his last game of rugby. The French club RC Toulon has confirmed that Nonu will leave them after rejoining the Top 14 outfit as injury cover.
item.content:encoded:
Former All Black and Hurricanes player Ma'a Nonu may have played his last game of rugby.

The French club RC Toulon has confirmed that Nonu will leave them after rejoining the Top 14 outfit as injury cover.

Nonu had two stints with Toulon following his retirement from Test rugby in 2015 and this season played a dozen games mostly off the bench.

"You will leave @RCTofficiel at the end of the season but you will always remain in the Red & Black Family," the club said in a statement .

Nonu was treated to a haka following his last game against European champions Bordeaux.

Nonu, 44, broke records as the oldest player in France's Top 14 competition and the oldest player to score a try in the competition.

Nonu played 103 tests for the All Blacks between 2003 and 2015 and won the World Cup in 2011 and 2015.

He played 126 games for the Hurricanes and also played in Japan, the United States and France.

He played four games for East Coast in provincial rugby in 2021.

item.title: FIFA to use new offside technology at 2026 World Cup
item.description: FIFA will introduce a new semi-automated offside technology at the 2026 FIFA World Cup aimed at speeding up offside decisions and

item.title: Paraguay return to FIFA World Cup after 16-year
item.description: After 16 years of waiting, Paraguay's passionate fans will finally hear their country's anthem at the 2026 FIFA World Cup once aga
